title: DBcompiler — Staged Lexer and Parser Hollowing
summary: Implementation-order DAG for the staged dbcompiler task. Each unit isolates one course-mainline helper or control-loop phase inside the existing lexer/parser Java files.
instructionPreview: This is a Java/Maven compiler-principles project that implements a complete lexical analyzer (lexer) and three parser front-ends — recursive-descent, LL(1) predictive, and LR table-driven — each producing three-address code (quadruples) for simple arithmetic expressions. Core methods across all modules have been replaced with stubs that throw `UnsupportedOperationException("TODO: implement")`.
instruction:
This is a Java/Maven compiler-principles project that implements a complete lexical analyzer (lexer) and three parser front-ends — recursive-descent, LL(1) predictive, and LR table-driven — each producing three-address code (quadruples) for simple arithmetic expressions. Core methods across all modules have been replaced with stubs that throw `UnsupportedOperationException("TODO: implement")`.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present. You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

The progressive requirements split the work into the following course-mainline stages:

- **Lexer — JudgeType** (`src/main/java/lexer/JudgeType.java`): Implement the keyword/delimiter membership helpers first, then the single- and double-delimiter type-code lookups that the lexer uses when emitting tokens. (~4 functions across 2 stages)

- **Lexer — WordScanner** (`src/main/java/lexer/WordScanner.java`): Implement the file-input path, the finite-automaton transition lookup, the main tokenization loop, and the output writer. The scanner must still handle integers, floats, scientific notation (e.g. `2e-2`), keywords, identifiers, single and double delimiters, single-line (`//`) and multi-line (`/* */`) comments, and unrecognized characters. (~4 functions across 4 stages)

- **Parser — LL(1) AnalysisTable** (`src/main/java/parser/LL1/AnalysisTable.java`): Build the LL(1) infrastructure in phases: extract terminal/non-terminal symbols, compute FIRST sets, implement FIRST/FOLLOW helper lookups, compute FOLLOW and SELECT sets, validate whether the grammar is LL(1), then finish the `initAll()` orchestration path and predictive parsing table. The class fields `Vn`, `Vt`, `first`, `follow`, `select`, `analysisTable`, and `ifNeedFollow` must all be correctly populated by the end. (~8 functions across 7 stages)

- **Parser — LL(1) Control** (`src/main/java/parser/LL1/LL1.java`): Implement the predictive parsing driver in stages: bootstrap the control loop and syntax stack, handle embedded action symbols `{G<op>}` / `{P<sym>}`, then complete the table-driven expansion path and acceptance logic. (~1 control method across 3 stages)

- **Parser — LR Control** (`src/main/java/parser/LR/LR.java`): Implement the SLR(1) analysis-table lookup first, then the shift/reduce control loop, and finally the reduce-step semantic actions that emit quadruples. (~2 functions across 3 stages)

- **Parser — Recursive Descent** (`src/main/java/parser/Recursive/Recursive.java`): Implement the entry setup in `beginToAnalysis()`, then the `E`, `T`, and `F` subroutines, and finally the `GEQ` quadruple-emission helper. The parser still reads characters from a `LinkedList`, uses a semantic stack (`SEM`), and produces `Quadruple` objects in `QT`. (~5 functions across 5 stages)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement them with these exact signatures:

- `lexer.WordScanner.getTextFromFileAndAnalysis()` — reads source code from `src/main/java/lexer/file/input.txt`, tokenizes it via the finite automaton, and populates the `tokens` list
- `lexer.WordScanner.writeToFile()` — writes all tokens to `src/main/java/lexer/file/output.txt`, one per line, in `<type, value>` format
- `lexer.JudgeType.isKeywords(String) -> boolean` — returns true if the string is a Java keyword
- `lexer.JudgeType.isDelimiter(Character) -> boolean` — returns true if the character is a delimiter
- `lexer.JudgeType.getDelimiterType(Character) -> int` — returns the type code for the delimiter, or -1 if not found
- `lexer.JudgeType.getDoubleDelimiterType(String) -> int` — returns the type code for a double delimiter, or -1 if not found
- `parser.Recursive.Recursive.beginToAnalysis(String) throws Exception` — entry point that initializes parser state, loads the input string, delegates to `E()`, and rejects expressions that do not end with `;`
- `parser.LL1.LL1.LL1Control(String) -> boolean` — LL(1) predictive parsing control; returns true on successful parse
- `parser.LR.LR.LRControl(String) -> boolean` — SLR(1) table-driven parsing control; returns true on accept
- `parser.LL1.AnalysisTable.initAll()` — initializes all LL(1) parsing data structures, including the `initAll()` orchestration/error-handling path (calls handleVnVt, handleFirst, handleFollow, handleSelect, handleAnalysisTable)
- `parser.LL1.AnalysisTable.judgeIfLL1() -> boolean` — returns true if the grammar is LL(1)

All three parsers operate on arithmetic expressions over single-letter identifiers and digits, with operators `+ - * /`, parentheses `( )`, and a terminating `;`. Expected output is a sequence of `Quadruple(operator, arg1, arg2, result)` objects where result temps are named `t1`, `t2`, ...

moduleDag.project: DBcompiler — Staged Lexer and Parser Hollowing
moduleDag.description: Implementation-order DAG for the staged dbcompiler task. Each unit isolates one course-mainline helper or control-loop phase inside the existing lexer/parser Java files.

nodes.label: Lexer — JudgeType membership helpers
nodes.description: Implement the linear scans that recognize Java keywords and single-character delimiters. This stage unblocks later delimiter-type lookup helpers and the lexer finite automaton.

nodes.label: Lexer — JudgeType delimiter type codes
nodes.description: Implement the type-code lookups for single and double delimiters so later lexer stages can emit the right token categories.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — symbol extraction
nodes.description: Populate the non-terminal and terminal sets from the grammar productions. This stage seeds every later LL(1) table-building phase.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — FIRST set construction
nodes.description: Compute each production’s FIRST set, including epsilon tracking and left-recursion rejection, so FOLLOW/SELECT generation has the right seeds.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — FIRST/FOLLOW helper lookups
nodes.description: Implement the recursive helper routines that query FIRST and FOLLOW information for grammar symbols. Later FOLLOW and table-building phases depend on them.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — FOLLOW set construction
nodes.description: Fill the FOLLOW sets for productions whose FIRST sets can derive epsilon, reusing the recursive helper routines from the previous stage.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — SELECT set construction
nodes.description: Build the SELECT set for every production by combining FIRST and FOLLOW information so the predictive table can be generated.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — grammar validation
nodes.description: Detect whether productions with the same left-hand side have intersecting SELECT sets, establishing whether the grammar is LL(1).

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — initAll orchestration and table build
nodes.description: Wire the LL(1) build pipeline together in `initAll()` and populate the predictive analysis table entries consumed by the LL(1) control loop.

nodes.label: Parser LR — analysis-table lookup
nodes.description: Map LR parser states and lookahead symbols to the correct action/goto cell in the hardcoded SLR(1) table.

nodes.label: Parser LR — shift/reduce dispatch loop
nodes.description: Build the outer LR control loop that advances input, consults the analysis table, and routes into shift, reduce, accept, or reject branches.

nodes.label: Parser LR — reduce semantics
nodes.description: Complete the reduce-step semantics so the LR parser pops handles, runs embedded actions, emits quadruples, and continues parsing.

nodes.label: Parser Recursive — analysis entry setup
nodes.description: Initialize recursive-descent parser state, load the input queue, advance the first lookahead symbol, and enforce the trailing semicolon check.

nodes.label: Parser Recursive — expression subroutine
nodes.description: Implement the `E()` production so recursive-descent parsing handles left-associative addition and subtraction.

nodes.label: Parser Recursive — term subroutine
nodes.description: Implement the `T()` production so multiplication and division bind tighter than additive operators.

nodes.label: Parser Recursive — factor subroutine
nodes.description: Implement the `F()` production so operands and parenthesized subexpressions are consumed correctly with appropriate parse errors.

nodes.label: Parser Recursive — quadruple emission
nodes.description: Finish recursive-descent parsing by implementing `GEQ()` so semantic actions emit three-address-code quadruples.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — control bootstrap
nodes.description: Initialize the LL(1) control loop, seed the syntax stack, read lookahead symbols, and route between terminal and non-terminal handling states.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — semantic action handling
nodes.description: Teach the LL(1) driver to execute `{G...}` and `{P...}` action symbols so quadruples and operand pushes can happen during parsing.

nodes.label: Parser LL(1) — predictive expansion loop
nodes.description: Complete the non-terminal expansion path so the LL(1) parser consults the analysis table, expands productions, and accepts or rejects correctly.

nodes.label: Lexer — WordScanner file input
nodes.description: Read the lexer input file line by line, skip whole-line comments early, queue characters for analysis, and trigger the finite automaton.

nodes.label: Lexer — WordScanner state transitions
nodes.description: Implement the finite-automaton transition lookup so each character class routes to the correct next state, including fallback acceptance states.

nodes.label: Lexer — WordScanner finite-automaton core
nodes.description: Implement the main lexical-analysis loop so numeric literals, identifiers, delimiters, comments, and unknown characters are classified into tokens.

nodes.label: Lexer — WordScanner output writer
nodes.description: Write the generated token stream to the lexer output file in the expected `<type, value>` format so the end-to-end lexer tests pass.

edges.label: Delimiter membership scans must exist before delimiter type codes are meaningful.
WordScanner transition classification relies on JudgeType delimiter helpers.
WordScanner token emission uses JudgeType keyword and delimiter-type lookups.
FIRST-set construction depends on the extracted terminal/non-terminal sets.
FOLLOW helper logic reuses the FIRST sets and epsilon bookkeeping.
FOLLOW-set construction depends on recursive follow/first helper lookups.
SELECT sets combine FIRST and FOLLOW information.
LL(1) validation compares SELECT sets for productions with the same left-hand side.
Table generation belongs after the grammar has full FIRST/FOLLOW/SELECT information.
The LL(1) parser must bootstrap against a populated analysis table.
The LR control loop depends on table lookups for every parser step.
Reduce-step semantic actions complete the LR control loop.
Entry setup must exist before expression recursion can run.
Expression parsing delegates into term parsing for precedence.
Term parsing delegates into factor parsing.
Quadruple emission finalizes the recursive-descent semantics after factors are parsed.
Action-symbol execution builds on the control-loop bootstrap.
Predictive expansion completes the LL(1) control loop after action handling exists.
The input loader feeds the finite automaton whose transitions must then be defined.
The lexer core relies on getNextState for every consumed character.
Token file output is only meaningful once the lexer core produces tokens.
